steps.title: Set the velocity boundary
steps.content: Before 2006, the estimates were low enough for the LMC to maintain orbit. The 2006 Kallivayalil estimate was too high. So the maximum velocity that would still allow orbit must be higher than the largest earlier working estimate but lower than the 2006 estimate.

steps.title: Use the bracketing values
steps.content: B and D put the maximum too low because they fall at or below earlier values that already allowed orbit. C is too high because 378 km/s was already too fast. The table brackets the maximum between 344 km/s and 378 km/s. The answer is A.
